title: Single-character alternations in regular expressions should be replaced with character classes
description: Using alternation (the | operator) for single characters in regular expressions is less efficient and less readable than using a character class. For example, the pattern (a|b|c) should be replaced with [abc]. This improves both performance and clarity of the regular expression.
recommendation: Replace single-character alternations with character classes in regular expressions.
impact: Improves performance and readability of regular expressions.

checks.message: Replace single-character alternations with character classes in regular expressions.
